compositional_truth_check.findings: P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is present. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are absent or have suffered total systemic loss, as the black pot containing the rabbit shows only a single Pothos leaf and no other foliage.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains soil but no visible String of Nickels foliage. Potential dormancy or total loss.
plant_audit.P3: Single Pothos leaf remains. The rabbit anchor is present. No other plant matter detected in this pot.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Zone: The black pot containing the rabbit shows no evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, appears to have organic debris (eggshell fragments) used as a soil amendment.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot; suggests an attempt at calcium supplementation or pest deterrent.

temporal_deltas.t4_to_t3: Pothos leaf remains static; no new growth.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: No significant change. The biome is in a state of stasis.

anomalies: Crushed eggshells in the black pot.
Absence of expected foliage in the yellow pot (P1).
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical desk environment shows a high degree of systemic loss. The Pothos (P3) is the only surviving specimen, represented by a single leaf. The yellow pot (P1) and the black pot (P2/P4) are effectively devoid of their registered occupants. The presence of eggshells indicates an intervention that has not yet resulted in visible vegetative recovery. The environment is currently static with no signs of active growth or decay progression over the 5-day observation window.
